evaluations.query: Set up a production Celery app with Redis broker
expected_behavior: Uses JSON serializer and never allows pickle deserialization
Sets task_acks_late=True to prevent task loss
Configures both task_time_limit and task_soft_time_limit for graceful termination
Sets worker_prefetch_multiplier=1 for fair task distribution across workers

evaluations.query: Route tasks to priority queues based on user tier
expected_behavior: Defines critical/high/default/low priority queues using kombu Queue declarations
Configures broker_transport_options with priority_steps for queue priority support
Implements TaskRouter class for dynamic routing
Assigns dedicated workers per queue with tuned concurrency

evaluations.query: Build an ETL pipeline with parallel processing and aggregation
expected_behavior: Uses chain for sequential extract-transform-load steps
Uses chord for parallel chunk processing with callback
Implements link_error for workflow-level error handling
Uses si() for immutable signatures in appropriate steps

evaluations.query: Implement a task with retry and idempotency protection
expected_behavior: Uses autoretry_for with retry_backoff=True and retry_jitter=True
Implements idempotency key check in Redis before processing
Sets max_retries with retry_backoff_max ceiling to bound delay growth
Moves permanently failed tasks to dead letter queue

evaluations.query: Configure periodic tasks with overlap prevention
expected_behavior: Uses celery.schedules.crontab for flexible time-based periodic scheduling
Implements Redis-based schedule lock to prevent overlap
Configures beat_schedule with explicit UTC timezone for consistency
Separates beat scheduler process from worker processes in production

evaluations.query: Add monitoring and health checks to Celery workers
expected_behavior: Connects to celery signals for task_prerun and task_failure
Implements health check verifying broker and active workers
Monitors queue depths for autoscaling decisions
Uses Flower with persistent storage for dashboard

evaluations.query: Track task progress with custom states
expected_behavior: Tracks job status updates via update_state with progress metadata
Uses update_state() for real-time progress reporting to clients
Queries progress via AsyncResult with state checks
Stores large results in S3/DB, not Redis

evaluations.query: Set up async background tasks with ARQ and FastAPI
expected_behavior: Creates ARQ worker with startup/shutdown lifecycle hooks
Enqueues jobs from FastAPI routes with enqueue_job()
Implements job status endpoint with Job.status()
Configures max_jobs and job_timeout in WorkerSettings

evaluations.query: Set up a Temporal activity for payment processing with proper heartbeat and retry configuration
expected_behavior: Implements activity heartbeat for long-running operations to report progress to Temporal
Classifies errors into retryable and non-retryable using ApplicationError with non_retryable flag
Configures start_to_close_timeout and heartbeat_timeout for activity timeout management
Sets up proper worker configuration with appropriate task queue and activity registration

evaluations.query: Define a Temporal workflow for order processing that handles signals and maintains determinism
expected_behavior: Keeps workflow code deterministic by avoiding random, datetime.now, and direct IO operations
Uses workflow.wait_condition for signal handling instead of direct state manipulation
Calls all IO operations through activities rather than executing them directly in workflow code
Implements saga pattern with compensating activities for multi-step transaction rollback

evaluations.query: My Celery task returns a 50MB DataFrame as the result and the Redis result backend keeps running out of memory
expected_behavior: Warns against storing large results in Redis as it is an in-memory store with size limits
Recommends storing large results in S3 or a database and returning only a reference key or URL
Sets result_expires to limit how long results are retained in the backend
Does not suggest increasing Redis memory as a fix for fundamentally oversized task results

evaluations.query: My Celery workers stop processing tasks after a Redis broker restart but show no errors in logs
expected_behavior: Configures broker_connection_retry_on_startup=True to automatically reconnect after broker restarts
Sets broker_transport_options with visibility_timeout to prevent lost tasks during reconnection
Implements a health check that verifies both broker connectivity and active worker count
Does not rely on worker logs alone since silent disconnection produces no error output

evaluations.query: Our API endpoint accepts a task_name parameter and calls app.send_task(task_name) to dispatch arbitrary Celery tasks
expected_behavior: Never allows user input to determine which Celery task is dispatched without validation
Uses an explicit allowlist of permitted task names mapped from user-facing action identifiers
Warns that arbitrary task dispatch enables remote code execution if custom tasks have side effects
Validates and sanitizes all task arguments before dispatching even for allowed task names
